changes_made: Removed k8s_service_mesh_one_way_traffic_control as it does not fit the naming pattern and is not a direct K8s resource.
review_notes: The control AC-4(7) is applicable to Kubernetes as it relates to enforcing one-way information flow, which can be implemented using NetworkPolicies. The function names for NetworkPolicies are mostly accurate but need slight adjustments for consistency. The service mesh function was removed due to inconsistency with the naming pattern and the focus on Kubernetes-native resources.
technical_concerns: Service mesh is not a direct Kubernetes resource and does not fit the naming convention.
naming_improvements: Ensure all function names strictly follow the k8s_<resource>_<check_description> pattern.
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | one - way flow mechanisms enforce one-way informa
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(7) focuses on enforcing one-way information flow. In Kubernetes, this can be achieved through NetworkPolicies that restrict traffic to one-way flows, either ingress or egress. Additionally, service meshes like Istio can enforce one-way traffic rules at the application layer, providing more granular control over traffic flows.
